title: Yosys output can not be disabled with pyosys
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

* Run the following python script:
```
from pyosys import libyosys as ys
```
The banner is shown (and there's no way to disable it)

* Run any command
```
from pyosys import libyosys as ys
d = ys.Design()
ys.run_pass("help", d)
```
The command output is shown (which is not always desirable)

* Run any command through the `tee -q` pass
```
from pyosys import libyosys as ys
d = ys.Design()
ys.run_pass("tee -q help", d)
```
The output of `help` is shown (which is not what happens in an interactive session)

## Comments
* While the C++ interface allows setting the output stream for logging, there's no way to do that using the python bindings (always uses stdout)
* Just having the banner is no big deal, but having `tee -q` to disable all the runtime output would be convenient, as long as there's no other python-exposed method for setting the log level
code_blocks: from pyosys import libyosys as ys

from pyosys import libyosys as ys
d = ys.Design()
ys.run_pass("help", d)

from pyosys import libyosys as ys
d = ys.Design()
ys.run_pass("tee -q help", d)
